_meta.purpose: Single source of truth for the LOVS snapshot's candidate target zones (the 'where could cases appear next' watch set). refresh_pipeline.py reads this so a future snapshot cannot silently miss a target. Targets are geography, not case assertions: adding a target asserts no cases, only that the zone is watched for onward spread.
_meta.ordering_note: Order is significant for the seeded Monte Carlo draw sequence. Append new targets at the end so earlier corridors' draws stay stable; do not reorder existing entries.
_meta.provenance_rule: A target needs a verified centroid in data/zones.json to render on the map, and a one-line rationale here. Geography only; case counts live in the manifest.

candidate_target_zones.provenance: Uganda border district on the Mpondwe/Bwera crossing toward Beni/North Kivu. Original 20 May target set.
Uganda capital; 2 imported BDBV cases (WHO PHEIC 17 May 2026). Original 20 May target set.
Uganda district adjacent to North Kivu across Lake Albert. Original 20 May target set.
North Kivu hub adjacent to Ituri. Original 20 May target set.

NW Uganda city on the Mahagi/Goli cross-border corridor, one of DRC's busiest Northern Corridor crossings. Geography knowable as of 20 May; added 21 May to close the documented Mahagi/Goli<->Arua blindspot. Centroid in data/zones.json.
NW Uganda district about 80km SE of Arua on the Pakwach-Nebbi-Arua road; same cross-border axis as Arua. Centroid in data/zones.json.
